profiles.hint: Optional — tailors your reward focus. No personal data is required.

RESIDENT.label: Resident
RESIDENT.reward: As a resident you earn the most for long-term local action — recurring clean-ups, water-quality monitoring and village recycling. Aim for the Eco Hero tier.

VISITOR.label: Visitor / Tourist
VISITOR.reward: As a visitor you earn quick rewards for low-threshold help during your stay — join a beach clean-up or report litter. Start with the Newcomer badge.

STUDENT.label: Pupil / Student
STUDENT.reward: As a pupil or student you earn extra for learning-by-doing — school solar, citizen-science monitoring and education projects.

VOLUNTEER.label: Volunteer
VOLUNTEER.reward: As a volunteer you earn for organising and leading — coordinate actions and bring others along. Aim for the Ambassador tier.

sdgGrid.heading: All 17 Sustainable Development Goals
sdgGrid.intro: ZOE maps its actions to the UN SDGs. Highlighted goals are addressed by ZOE projects; select any goal to open its official UN page.
sdgGrid.openOnUn: Open SDG {{number}} on the UN website

3.title: Good Health and Well-Being
3.contribution: Healthy ecosystems and clean environments contribute to community well-being.

4.title: Quality Education
4.contribution: Environmental education programmes build long-term sustainability literacy.

6.title: Clean Water and Sanitation
6.contribution: Protecting water sources and coastal zones ensures clean water for all.

8.title: Decent Work and Economic Growth
8.contribution: Sustainable tourism and local green initiatives support decent work and the regional economy.

11.title: Sustainable Cities and Communities
11.contribution: Green urban planning and community engagement foster sustainable local governance.

12.title: Responsible Consumption and Production
12.contribution: Circular economy and waste-reduction actions promote sustainable patterns.

13.title: Climate Action
13.contribution: Local climate mitigation and adaptation measures address the climate crisis.

14.title: Life Below Water
14.contribution: Protecting coastal and marine ecosystems is central to Corfu's environment.

15.title: Life on Land
15.contribution: Biodiversity conservation and reforestation protect terrestrial ecosystems.

17.title: Partnerships for the Goals
17.contribution: Multi-stakeholder collaboration is essential to achieving all other goals.

active-projects.label: Active Projects
active-projects.description: Environmental action projects currently running or recently completed under the ZOE programme.

citizens-engaged.label: Citizens Engaged
citizens-engaged.description: Unique participants across all ZOE project activities, events and monitoring programmes (cumulative).

waste-diverted.label: Waste Diverted
waste-diverted.description: Solid waste diverted from landfill through recycling and composting programmes.

coastline-monitored.label: Coastline Monitored
coastline-monitored.description: Kilometres of Northern Corfu coastline covered by regular ZOE litter monitoring and cleanup programmes.

habitats-restored.label: Habitats Restored
habitats-restored.description: Terrestrial and wetland habitat restored or under active restoration management.

ghg-avoided.label: GHG Avoided
ghg-avoided.description: Estimated greenhouse-gas emissions avoided through organic-waste diversion (composting programme).

students-reached.label: Students Reached
students-reached.description: Students in Northern Corfu primary schools currently receiving structured environmental education through ZOE.

sdgs-addressed.label: SDGs Addressed
sdgs-addressed.description: UN Sustainable Development Goals directly addressed by one or more ZOE project activities.

submit-idea.title: Submit an Idea
submit-idea.description: Have an idea for a new environmental initiative in your community? Share it with the ZOE team. All ideas are reviewed by the municipal coordination committee.

volunteer.title: Volunteer
volunteer.description: Lend your time, skills or local knowledge to an active project. Opportunities range from field restoration work to data entry, event organisation and school presentations.

join-event.title: Join an Event
join-event.description: Attend one of the regular ZOE events: cleanup days, biodiversity walks, workshops, community forums, and more. Family-friendly options available.

report-issue.title: Report an Environmental Issue
report-issue.description: Spotted illegal dumping, water pollution, or habitat damage? Report it through the platform and the municipality will be notified. Each report is tracked and responded to.

feedback.title: Give Feedback
feedback.description: Tell us what you think about the ZOE programme, which projects matter most to you, or how we can improve citizen participation. Your input shapes future iterations.

prototype.banner: Research Prototype — This platform contains fictional data for academic purposes (DSR, FAU SoSe 2026). Not a real municipal service.

projects.title: ZOE Projects
projects.subtitle: 31 environmental initiatives for Northern Corfu
projects.joinSuccess: You joined the project and earned {{points}} points!
projects.alreadyParticipating: You are already participating in this project.

rewards.heroSubtitle: Every action you take for Northern Corfu earns points. Rise through five Greek-named tiers — from Σπόρος (Seed) to Θεματοφύλακας (Steward) — and unlock real recognition from the municipality and local community.
rewards.demoText: The tracker below shows a sample participant at {{points}} points. In the live platform, this reflects your real activity log.
rewards.highestTier: You have reached the highest tier. Thank you, Θεματοφύλακας!
rewards.tiersSubtitle: Each tier unlocks real, tangible recognition from the municipality and local partners.
rewards.tiersNote: All rewards listed are indicative for this prototype and subject to formal programme approval.
rewards.earnSubtitle: Points are awarded for verified participation. Every contribution counts.
rewards.earnNote: Points are illustrative. Final values will be confirmed with municipality partners.
rewards.milestonesSubtitle: When the whole community reaches a goal together, everyone benefits. These shared milestones reward collective action.
rewards.milestonesNote: Milestone progress shown here is illustrative dummy data.

why.title: Low cost to the municipality
why.desc: Most rewards are recognition, certificates, and partnerships with local producers — not cash. The stewardship map and annual report cost almost nothing to operate.

why.title: Grounded in local culture
why.desc: Greek names and tangible local rewards (olive oil, compost, named map markers) resonate with Corfu residents far more than generic digital badges.

why.title: Encourages long-term participation
why.desc: Monthly stewardship points and co-design access at the top tier give residents ongoing reasons to stay engaged — not just one-off event attendance.

dashboard.title: My Dashboard
dashboard.noActivity: No activity yet. Join a project to get started!

participate.title: Citizen Participation
participate.subtitle: The ZOE programme is driven by citizens as much as by the municipality. There are many ways to get involved — from sharing a local idea to volunteering in the field. Choose how you want to participate below.
participate.rewardsLink: Every action earns ZOE points — see the Rewards Programme

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.subtitle: ZOE Platform — Municipality of Northern Corfu
accessibility.conformanceText: This website partially conforms to WCAG 2.1 Level AA as required by EU Directive 2016/2102 and the European Accessibility Act (EAA, 2025). Some areas are still in active development as part of this research prototype.
accessibility.limitsText: As this is a research prototype (DSR artefact, FAU SoSe 2026), some features are not yet fully optimised for assistive technologies. Known limitations include participation option texts currently available in English only.
accessibility.feedbackText: We welcome feedback on the accessibility of this platform. If you experience any barriers, please contact us:
accessibility.legalBasisText: This accessibility statement is made pursuant to EU Directive 2016/2102 on the accessibility of websites and mobile applications of public sector bodies, transposed via EN 301 549.

hero.title: ZOE — Environmental Actions for Northern Corfu
hero.subtitle: A transparent, participatory platform connecting citizens with the environmental projects protecting the nature, coastlines, and communities of Northern Corfu.

stats.disclaimer: * All statistics are prototype dummy data for demonstration purposes.

pillars.heading: Why the ZOE Platform?
pillars.subheading: Environmental governance in small municipalities often lacks visibility and citizen connection. ZOE changes that with three core commitments.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: Every project publishes progress metrics, participation numbers, and impact data openly. Citizens can see where funding goes and what is being achieved.

participation.title: Citizen Participation
participation.description: From submitting ideas to volunteering on the ground, the ZOE platform connects residents directly to the environmental actions shaping their community.

impact.title: Sustainability Impact
impact.description: Aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, each ZOE project is designed to deliver measurable, long-term environmental benefit.

featured.heading: Active Projects
featured.subheading: Environmental actions underway right now in Northern Corfu.

cta.heading: Your participation matters
cta.body: Whether you have five minutes to share feedback or five hours to volunteer at a restoration site — the ZOE programme needs your involvement to succeed.
cta.rewards: Every action earns ZOE points — rise from Σπόρος (Seed) to Θεματοφύλακας (Steward) and unlock recognition from the municipality.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: ZOE Platform — Municipality of Northern Corfu
privacy.prototypeNotice: This is a research prototype. No real personal data is collected or processed.
privacy.overviewText: The ZOE platform is a Design Science Research prototype developed for academic purposes (FAU SoSe 2026). It does not collect, store, or process any real personal data. All user accounts and platform data are fictional seed data used solely for demonstration purposes.
privacy.dataText: The prototype uses fictional seed data (names, email addresses, participation records) generated for testing purposes. Any data entered during the demonstration is stored locally in a development database and is not shared with third parties.
privacy.cookiesText: The platform uses localStorage to persist language preference and authentication state within the browser. No tracking cookies are set. No third-party analytics are used.
privacy.rightsText: Under GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify, and erase your data. As this is a prototype with fictional data, these rights are exercised by contacting the research team.
privacy.processorsText: Where this prototype is operated as a live demo, the following processors may be used under data-processing agreements (to be concluded by the operator, EU region): Supabase (database & file-storage hosting) and DeepL (machine translation of project texts). No data is shared with these services beyond what is technically required to provide the platform.
privacy.analyticsText: If web analytics are enabled, this prototype uses a privacy-friendly, cookieless analytics service that sets no cookies, collects no personal data and does not track individuals across sites. Only aggregated, anonymous usage statistics (e.g. page views) are recorded. Analytics are disabled by default in this prototype.

getInvolved.title: Get Involved
getInvolved.subtitle: Whether you live in Northern Corfu or are visiting for a week — there is a way for you to help protect it. Explore ZOE's initiatives, see how visitors can contribute, and stay informed.

initiatives.heading: Explore initiatives by theme
initiatives.intro: ZOE bundles the municipality's environmental actions into four thematic initiatives. Choose a theme to see the related actions.

marineCoast.name: Marine & Coast
marineCoast.description: Protecting Northern Corfu's 22 km coastline and freshwater streams — beach cleanups, marine litter monitoring and water quality.

natureBiodiversity.name: Nature & Biodiversity
natureBiodiversity.description: Safeguarding natural monuments — the Antinioti Lagoon, the Erimitis Peninsula and traditional olive landscapes.

circularClimate.name: Circular & Climate
circularClimate.description: Cutting waste and closing loops — recycling hubs and community composting across the villages.

educationTourism.name: Education & Tourism
educationTourism.description: Building awareness — environmental education in schools and a sustainable tourism scheme.

tourists.heading: How visitors can contribute
tourists.intro: Northern Corfu welcomes around four million visitors a year. Tourism is often seen only as a source of waste — but visitors can also be part of the solution.

cleanups.title: Join a beach cleanup
cleanups.desc: Spend an hour at a monthly cleanup in Kassiopi, Sidari or Palaiokastritsa.

report.title: Report litter or pollution
report.desc: Flag a litter hotspot or a pollution incident so the municipality can act.

choose.title: Choose certified operators
choose.desc: Favour accommodation and tours committed to ZOE's sustainable tourism standards.

respect.title: Respect nature & wildlife
respect.desc: Keep your distance from turtle nesting sites and protected wetlands.

tourists.evidenceNote: Evidence from comparable Mediterranean destinations shows that involving visitors and communities through digital tools measurably improves a destination's sustainability (Vegas Macias et al., 2023; Laksmi et al., 2026).

newsletter.heading: Stay informed
newsletter.intro: Get occasional updates on new actions, events and results in Northern Corfu.
newsletter.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
newsletter.success: Thanks! In the full version you would now receive a confirmation email.
newsletter.prototypeNotice: Prototype: no email is stored or sent. This illustrates the planned newsletter opt-in.

sdgDashboard.title: SDG Dashboard
sdgDashboard.intro: The ZOE programme aligns all projects to relevant UN Sustainable Development Goals. This dashboard shows which goals are addressed, how many projects contribute to each, and illustrative progress estimates.
sdgDashboard.prototypeNote: All percentages and figures are fictional dummy data for demonstration purposes only.
sdgDashboard.aboutText: SDG alignment is self-reported by project coordinators and reviewed by the ZOE team. Progress percentages represent the municipality's own assessment against locally-defined targets. This dashboard is a prototype — a real system would pull live data from verified project reports.

transparency.title: Impact & Transparency Data
transparency.intro: The ZOE programme publishes transparent data on all project activities and outcomes. This page provides a programme-wide overview of key performance indicators.
transparency.prototypeNotice: All figures below are fictional dummy data for demonstration purposes. A real deployment would show verified, live data from project reports.

principles.title: Open Progress Data
principles.desc: Every project publishes regular updates on progress, spending, and outcomes. No data is hidden from citizens.

principles.title: Independent Metrics
principles.desc: Where possible, progress is measured by third-party organisations or citizen scientists rather than project teams alone.

principles.title: Public Participation Records
principles.desc: Participation counts and event records are published openly, showing who is engaged and how.

principles.title: SDG Accountability
principles.desc: All SDG alignment claims are justified with specific, measurable targets and reviewed annually.

principles.title: Feedback Integration
principles.desc: Citizen feedback is tracked and visibly incorporated into project iteration decisions.

principles.title: Annual Reporting
principles.desc: The ZOE programme publishes an annual report combining all project data, citizen engagement statistics, and environmental outcomes.

transparency.footerText: This is a prototype platform built for a Design Science Research academic project. All data shown is fictional and intended to demonstrate the information architecture and UX concepts only. A production deployment would require a verified data pipeline from real project management systems.

audiences.title: Who We Reach — Target Audiences & Sensitization
audiences.intro: Environmental change in Northern Corfu requires reaching very different groups of people — each with distinct concerns, channels, and motivations. ZOE uses a multi-audience, multi-channel approach because no single message or medium works for everyone.
audiences.researchNote: This analysis is based on demographic research into Northern Corfu and the Ionian islands, civic engagement studies in Greek municipalities, and fieldwork by the Ionian Environment Foundation (IEF). The platform itself is a prototype — not yet live with real reach data.

context.label: permanent residents on Corfu
context.note: majority in working age, ageing rapidly

context.label: visitors per year
context.note: 40× the permanent population in peak season

principles.title: Local and concrete
principles.desc: Name real places — Antinioti Lagoon, Erimitis, Kassiopi beach. Abstract 'sustainability' messaging fails where tangible, local threats succeed.

principles.title: Multi-channel by design
principles.desc: Digital-only misses 40%+ of Northern Corfu's rural population. Every programme must include offline channels: radio, community meetings, church networks.

principles.title: Seasonal timing
principles.desc: Launch engagement in shoulder season (April–May, September–October). Summer is for tourists and working — not for launching new civic programmes to residents.

principles.title: Trusted intermediaries
principles.desc: In village settings, a respected local farmer or fisherman is more persuasive than municipal communications. Identify and support these natural advocates.

principles.title: Visible results first
principles.desc: Post-crisis trust in Greek municipalities is low. Show tangible results — a cleaned beach, a restored terrace wall, a saved forest — before asking for sustained commitment.

principles.title: Frame around livelihood, not ideology
principles.desc: For farmers, fishermen and businesses, sustainability must be an economic argument before it is a moral one. Show how it protects income, not just the environment.

audiences.ctaText: Participate in the ZOE programme — whichever group you belong to, there is a role for you.

events.title: Events & Initiatives
events.intro: Upcoming events, workshops, cleanup days and community forums. Find an activity near you and get involved.
events.prototypeNote: Prototype: all event data and dates are illustrative dummy data.
events.suggestText: Want to suggest a new event or initiative?
events.suggestLink: Submit your idea on the Participation page →

roadmap.title: Platform Roadmap
roadmap.intro: The ZOE platform follows an iterative Design Science Research process. This roadmap describes the planned phases from the current MVP prototype through to a full production system.
roadmap.statusNote: Phase 1 (MVP Prototype) is complete. Future phases depend on evaluation findings and municipality decisions.
roadmap.backendText: The technical design for the future backend (Phases 4–5) is documented in the project repository. It covers database schema, API layer, admin interface, and security/privacy considerations.

phases.description: Build and demonstrate the core frontend prototype as a Design Science Research artefact. Establish information architecture, dummy data, and core participation workflows.
items: Citizen Participation page with mock form

phases.description: Improve UI polish, content quality and accessibility based on initial user feedback. Conduct formative evaluation with municipality staff and citizen focus groups.
items: Content reviewed by municipality communications team

phases.description: Conduct structured evaluation against DSR criteria. Iterate on findings. Prepare evaluation report for academic submission and stakeholder presentation.
items: Platform iteration based on evaluation findings
Updated artifact description and architecture docs
Formal evaluation report for academic submission

phases.description: Design and prototype a real backend to replace dummy data. Establish database schema, API layer, admin interface, and citizen submission workflows.
items: Database schema design (projects, events, users, submissions)
Data migration from dummy to real data
Pilot deployment with selected municipality staff

phases.description: Full production deployment for public use by Northern Corfu residents and municipality. Ongoing maintenance, analytics, and further feature development.
items: API integrations (e.g., municipal systems, open data)

about.heroSubtitle: ZOE (Ζωή — "life" in Greek) is the strategic environmental action framework of the Municipality of Northern Corfu. This digital platform is the communication and participation layer of the ZOE programme, built as a Design Science Research artefact.
about.whatP1: ZOE is a strategic framework for environmental governance at the municipal level. It coordinates a portfolio of environmental projects spanning biodiversity, circular economy, waste reduction, water protection, education, and sustainable tourism.
about.whatP2: The programme is grounded in the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and European Green Deal principles, adapted for the specific ecological and social context of Northern Corfu.
about.whatP3: Key to the ZOE approach is the belief that environmental action cannot succeed without genuine citizen participation and radical transparency. This platform is designed to enable both.

principles.title: Transparency
principles.desc: Open progress data for every project

about.dsrIntro: This platform is a DSR artefact — a prototype designed to address a real-world problem through a structured research process. It follows the Peffers et al. (2007) DSR methodology framework.

dsrSteps.title: Problem Identification
dsrSteps.description: Northern Corfu faces environmental degradation, low citizen awareness of municipal sustainability efforts, and limited transparent communication channels between government and residents.

dsrSteps.title: Define Objectives
dsrSteps.description: Design a digital platform artifact that increases transparency of environmental actions, enables citizen participation, and communicates SDG alignment — all accessible to non-technical users.

dsrSteps.title: Design & Development
dsrSteps.description: Build a frontend MVP prototype using React + TypeScript + Tailwind. Structure information around projects, SDG mapping, events and citizen engagement. Use realistic dummy data.

dsrSteps.title: Demonstration
dsrSteps.description: This prototype demonstrates the artifact to stakeholders — municipality staff, researchers and citizens — showing the intended information architecture and participation workflows.

dsrSteps.title: Evaluation
dsrSteps.description: An evaluation plan (see docs/) defines how the platform should be assessed against criteria: usefulness, usability, completeness, accessibility and participation support.

dsrSteps.title: Communication
dsrSteps.description: Results, design decisions, and lessons learned are documented in DSR methodology, architecture and evaluation documents for academic and stakeholder communication.

about.researchNote: This platform is a prototype (Version 1). Further iterations should follow user testing, expert evaluation, and stakeholder feedback as described in the evaluation plan.

issues.title: Waste crisis
issues.desc: Corfu's only landfill at Temploni filled to capacity and was blockaded in 2018, creating a visible garbage crisis during peak tourist season. A new waste treatment unit is planned for 2027.

issues.title: Olive grove abandonment
issues.desc: The post-1960s shift to tourism left thousands of hectares of terraced olive groves in Northern Corfu's hill villages abandoned, accelerating soil erosion and fire risk.

issues.title: Antinioti Lagoon under pressure
issues.desc: The Natura 2000 protected lagoon between Kassiopi and Roda faces agricultural runoff and habitat degradation. It supports 44+ migratory bird species.

issues.title: Erimitis development threat
issues.desc: A major hotel and marina development proposed for the last virgin forested headland in NE Corfu has triggered the most significant civic environmental campaign in recent Corfu history.

about.partnersTitle: Existing partners & initiatives ZOE builds on

partners.name: Ionian Environment Foundation (IEF)
partners.desc: Island-wide environmental charity funding 18+ projects including Posidonia mapping, marine cleanup and school education.

partners.name: SIN.PRAXI — Sinies Small Green Spot
partners.desc: Community cooperative in NE Corfu running the only licensed recycling hub on the island. ZOE replicates this model.

partners.name: Save Erimitis Coalition
partners.desc: Citizen campaign opposing development on the Erimitis Peninsula — a model for community-led environmental advocacy.

partners.name: All For Blue
partners.desc: NGO conducting seasonal beach and underwater cleanups at Palaiokastritsa and other Corfu beaches.
